title: Shift in CPC Crude Exports Towards Suezmax Utilisation
section_path: Shift in CPC Crude Exports Towards Suezmax Utilisation
section_path_text: Shift in CPC Crude Exports Towards Suezmax Utilisation
summary:
Main
Tanker - Weekly Market Monitor
Snapshot of Crude and Product Freight Rates, Supply-Demand
Week 20, 16 May, 2025
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-05-16 125845.png | assets/2025-05-16_shift-in-cpc-crude-exports-towards-suezmax-utilisation_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_988458480f33.png
Shift in CPC Crude Exports Towards Suezmax Utilisation
The 2025 outlook for crude oil exports from the CPC terminal at Novorossiysk indicates a decisive structural shift toward Suezmax utilisation, with a marked decline in Aframax deployment. Data visualisations from Signal Ocean reveal that Q1 2025 recorded the highest quarterly volume of Suezmax loadings across the past four years, significantly surpassing figures for 2022 through 2024. This evolving vessel preference appears driven by both logistical efficiency and shifting trade dynamics.
